claim: A laser‑powder‑bed‑fused Ti45Zr45Nb10 β‑phase solid solution is expected to exhibit a Young’s modulus of ≈45 GPa and a 0 K yield strength of ≈900 MPa, matching cortical bone stiffness while doubling its strength.
An equiatomic Al‑Fe‑Cr‑Ti‑Mo high‑entropy alloy is expected to retain >80 % of its 1 GPa yield strength at 600 °C because of sluggish diffusion and coherent B2 precipitates that hinder creep.

The perovskite-derived oxycarbide Sr₂TaO₃C is suggested to exhibit an indirect bandgap exceeding 2.5 eV and a conduction-band edge insufficiently positioned to drive hydrogen evolution, making it unlikely to support efficient visible-light water-splitting applications.

A high-pressure bcc phase of CaB₆ is suggested to exhibit good electron-phonon coupling (λ ≈ 0.3) and phonon frequencies (ωlog ≈ 150 K), resulting in a negligible superconducting Tc (<1 K) even at extreme pressures of 40 GPa.
Layered nickel oxyfluoride LaNiOF is suggested to exhibit ambient‑pressure superconductivity with an estimated Tc of 10–12 K due to pronounced Fermi-surface nesting.
Argyrodite Li₆PS₅Br0.5Cl0.5 adopts the F‑43m structure and shows predicted room‑temperature Li‑ion conductivity of ≈18 mS cm⁻¹ with a migration barrier of 0.20 eV.
Disordered rock‑salt Li1.2Ti0.4Mn0.4O2 is suggested to deliver only ~150 mAh g⁻¹ reversible capacity at an average voltage of 2.0 V due to incomplete Mn redox activation and poor lithium transport.
